qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Admin syndication mapping fetch writes attacker-controlled PHP into web-reachable files/ and enables code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is a stored/blind XSS in the BazaR form title field (`bf_titre`) rendered on the `/?BazaR&vue=consulter` path, while this finding targets admin-triggered syndication enclosure download in `tools/syndication/actions/SyndicationAction.php` that writes a `.php` file under `files/` for server-side code execution.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only qualifying finding is a syndication-based server-side file-write/RCE chain and does not identify the advisory's BazaR title-field stored XSS root cause.
evidence: The advisory describes persistent blind XSS in the BazaR form title field, triggered when `/?BazaR&vue=consulter` renders stored attacker input.
The repo snapshot renders the BazaR title field with `{{ value|raw }}` in `tools/bazar/templates/fields/title.twig`, which is the advisory's affected component family.
The session finding instead traces attacker-controlled feed data through `tools/syndication/actions/SyndicationAction.php` into `downloadFile()`, preserving a `.php` basename under `files/`.

The finding's PoC report verifies `GET /files/<sha1>_shell.php?proof=1` returning `EVOHUNT_PWNED`, which proves PHP execution from a dropped file rather than JavaScript execution from a stored title field.
